differential_diagnoses: Infective endocarditis (Staphylococcus aureus) with septic emboli
Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with Libman-Sacks endocarditis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Disseminated intravascular coagulation secondary to occult malignancy or infection
Primary angiitis of the central nervous system
next_steps: Transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to assess for valvular vegetations
Antiphospholipid antibody panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin antibodies, anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I)
Consultation with rheumatology and infectious disease specialists
Repeat blood cultures and specialized serological testing for Rickettsia and Leptospira
Continued broad-spectrum antimicrobial and hemodynamic support in the ICU
